body:
status=blocked
reason=Host-level /tmp exhaustion (ENOSPC) is breaking the Claude Code Bash tool's own sandbox on this machine, not just the ddx binary under test. Every Bash invocation fails immediately with `ENOSPC: no space left on device, mkdir '/tmp/claude-501/<worktree-name>'`, before any command output — confirmed with 4 retries (`true`, `echo hi`, `ls /tmp`, and `ls /tmp` with dangerouslyDisableSandbox), all identical failures. Read/Edit/Write tools remain functional (direct filesystem access, no Bash sandbox), so I read execute_bead_loop_cleanup_test.go and execution_cleanup.go to understand the fix, but this bead's AC #3 and #4 require `go test` and `lefthook run pre-commit`, both of which need a working Bash shell. I cannot verify a code change without Bash, and the project's testing policy prohibits committing unverified/red code. This is a recurring host condition documented in memory (project_worker_bash_sandbox_tmp_enospc.md) — this is at least the 6th occurrence today across unrelated beads on this host. The host needs /tmp disk/inode cleanup or the harness scratch dir needs relocating off /tmp before any bead requiring Bash can execute on this machine.
suggested_action=recheck the external blocker and move status to open when cleared

no_changes
rationale: status: blocked

Read/Edit/Write tools remain functional (direct filesystem access, no Bash sandbox), so I read execute_bead_loop_cleanup_test.go and execution_cleanup.go to understand the fix, but this bead's AC #3 and #4 require `go test` and `lefthook run pre-commit`, both of which need a working Bash shell. I cannot verify a code change without Bash, and the project's testing policy prohibits committing unverified/red code. This is a recurring host condition documented in memory (project_worker_bash_sandbox_tmp_enospc.md) — this is at least the 6th occurrence today across unrelated beads on this host. The host needs /tmp disk/inode cleanup or the harness scratch dir needs relocating off /tmp before any bead requiring Bash can execute on this machine.
